articles.title: Israel's top court upholds detention of Gaza hospital director without charge
articles.summary: Israel's top court upholds detention of Gaza hospital director without charge Israel's Supreme Court has rejected an appeal by prominent Palestinian doctor Hussam Abu Safiya, ordering that he remain detained without charge under Israel's Unlawful Combatants Law. Abu Safiya, who has been held by Israeli authorities since December 2024, is being detained at Nafha Prison, where his lawyer says he has been subjected to solitary confinement and denied adequate medical care. Israeli authorities accuse the former Gaza hospital director of having links to Hamas but have not publicly presented evidence

articles.title: Death toll in Lebanon reaches 3,826 as Israeli strikes continue
articles.summary: Death toll in Lebanon reaches 3,826 as Israeli strikes continue Israel has launched three consecutive drone strikes on the Mayfadoun town square in southern Lebanon’s Nabitieh district, Lebanon’s National News Agency reported. The report added that the Israeli army dropped a sound bomb in Haddatha which injured four people. Lebanon’s health ministry has revised the death toll in Lebanon on Tuesday to 3,826, with 11,851 wounded since the conflict began on 2 March.

articles.summary: US-Iran deal signing scheduled for Friday in Switzerland The Swiss foreign ministry said that the US-Iran memorandum of understanding is currently scheduled for signing on Friday at the Burgenstock resort in central Switzerland. In contrast to the justifications for Israeli victory over Iran pushed by pro-war pundits, "Israel has been dealt a crushing defeat of historic proportions", according to Dabashi, arguing that instead of weakening the Islamic Republic, Iran is instead "stronger than it was before". articles.title: Trump ‘wouldn’t mind’ sending Iran deal to Congress
articles.summary: Trump ‘wouldn’t mind’ sending Iran deal to Congress Trump said on Tuesday that a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Iran has been “all signed” and the Strait of Hormuz will be “completely open” by Friday. He added that he will go over the specifics of the MoU with the media in the next couple of days. When asked whether he would be open to sending the agreement to Congress for review, the US president said that he liked the idea and “wouldn’t mind” doing so.

articles.summary: Rights groups call for a halt of AI tech use in the military Rights groups and tech activists have warned against the increasing use of AI in the military, specifically citing Israel's genocide in Gaza as a dangerous precedent. In a joint statement signed by more than a hundred people and organisations including Amnesty International, Access Now and the Stop Killer Robots Campaign, they warned that the expansion of AI in military "kill chains" risked greater civilian bloodshed and a lack of accountability.
